content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule. Looking at atom C:11 (carbon with index 11), I can identify its neighbor atoms by examining the covalent bonds connected to it:

From the image, atom C:11 is directly bonded to:
- H:13 (hydrogen atom with index 13) - visible as a white sphere connected to C:11
- C:14 (carbon atom with index 14) - visible as an orange sphere connected to C:11
- S:10 (sulfur atom with index 10) - visible as a yellow sphere connected to C:11

These three atoms are covalently connected to C:11.

Regarding the geomconf_index, based on the file naming convention in the working directory, I can see "geomconf_8617.xyz", which suggests the geomconf_index is 8617.

Let me confirm this by checking if there's any additional information in the files, but from the visual evidence and file naming, I'm confident in my identification.

The neighbor atoms of atom C:11 are:
- Atom index 13, element H
- Atom index 14, element C  
- Atom index 10, element S

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 8617.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 11, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
